title: Building AI Agents for Enterprise Operations
summary: Anish Acharya and Olivia Moore speak with Pablo Palafox and Luis Paarup about the challenges of deploying AI agents in operationally complex industries. The conversation covers the evolution of voice AI, enterprise workflows, and why logistics became an early proving ground for agent-based systems. They discuss context, coordination, and execution inside large organizations, as well as the role of forward-deployed engineering, enterprise deployment, and what it takes to move AI from experimentation into production.
score_reason: The discussion on building AI agents for complex enterprise operations offers high content potential with tactical insights and practitioner perspectives.
partner_takeaway: Deploying AI agents in complex enterprise operations requires addressing challenges in context, coordination, and execution to improve workflows effectively.
partner_summary: Experts discuss the evolution and deployment of AI agents in operationally complex industries like logistics, highlighting challenges and solutions around context and coordination. This is highly relevant for enterprise leaders and AI practitioners aiming to automate workflows and improve operational efficiency. Readers should explore agent-based AI approaches tailored to their organizational complexities and use cases.

title: A rational conversation on where AI is actually going | Benedict Evans
